reviews.review_description: You can tell the dev put a ton of love and attention into this game. It was a blast from start to finish. and great tropical vibes to!
It starts slow but after a couple chapters I found myself mesmerized by the landscape and mystical architecture. A beautiful adventure game with decent puzzles.

reviews.review_title: Im Stuck after you get out of the Dream
reviews.review_description: I Didn't know there at this was the wrong game that I was trying to buy.It's where you're stranded on the island And I don't like this game that much Could you please send back my money?
Fun to play,

Great puzzle adventure. True, graphics got quite a downgrade compared to flat version and the game never got the Quest 3 update, but with the help of Quest Games Optimizer, the game looks very good on Quest 3. It gets better and better the more you play and the scale is wonderful in VR.

Puzzles are good. Not too easy, but also not too hard. While they don't quite reach the heights of VR's best puzzlers, like Red Matter 2, The Room VR, The 7th Guest, Retropolis 2, etc., they're still satisfying to solve.

Story is excellent and if you're a fan of Lovecraftian stories, you'll gonna love this one (it's not the usual horror, but more of a mystery adventure).

I recommend this game a lot to all adventure games fans. But play it with Quest Games Optimizer.
Puzzles were not fun but if you like story then might be worth playing.

I have just bought Red Matter 2, Into the Radius and Call of the Sea for the Quest 3. Trying out this game after Red Matter 2  was a big mistake! Ok, RM 2 (2022) is a fresher one, but call of the sea (2021) is released 3 years after RM 1 (2018) but looks like as it was released 3 years before! 
Just wanted to start a refund process but found an uploadvr article, and maybe the game will have a Quest 3 enhancement. So waiting till next weekend with the refund and if the enhancement is released I'll update my review. But for Quest 2 owners - still a big no!

A couple of months ago, I played the non-VR version of Call of the Sea, and I couldn't help but think how incredible it would be to experience this game in virtual reality. So, when I discovered that there was a VR version available, I was genuinely excited. However, upon starting the game on my Quest 3, I was immediately disappointed with the visual quality.

Unfortunately, the VR version of Call of the Sea suffers from some visual issues that detract from the overall experience. I noticed pop-ups, flickering, lack of anti-aliasing, and low-detailed objects, which adversely affected the immersion and atmosphere of this otherwise beautiful story-driven game. These technical shortcomings can be quite distracting and take away from the otherwise well-implemented gameplay.

It’s a great introduction to cosmic horror with a tone down level due to the choice of the art style. The point and click puzzle levels are an average level of difficulty which make the game a very short experience.The voice actors are good, however the direction is lacking and some performances sometime are lacking in emotional impact.The last third of the game feels rushed because of the uneven puzzle difficulty  and story narrative. I can tell that there was a longer playthrough intended for the game. Good adaptation to VR.

reviews.review_title: This is a very special game
reviews.review_description:
I had played it on PC, I was looking forward to try the VR version and I feel the same desire as if I saw it for the first time. 

Call Of The Sea, more than a game, is a gift for lovers of puzzles and good stories told in an immersive, colorful and exotic environment.

It has something of Lovecraft, but not from the point of a horror title or the typical descent into madness of its characters. But it is a mysterious approach to what the ocean hides.

The adventure could be lonely, since our protagonist has set out alone on this journey, but far from that, the feeling produced by the scenarios is familiar and comfortable. Also, the music accompanies in a great way at specific moments.

Personally, I tend to get dizzy with the control in most games, but in this case the handling of the character is smooth and light.

Trust me, it's a place you'll want to come back to.

See you at sea

developer_response.body: Thanks a million for the raving review Miriamne,  we worked hard to create an immersive experience and are happy to hear you dig it!

reviews.review_description: I love adventure games and story games with puzzles to solve. This one didn’t do it for me. I’m 3/4 done and just want to be done with it. ￼Definitely not worth a second play through.￼

I’m a big “escape game” fan. This game had a good plot and was easy to move around. The mystery aspect, graphics, and move around was top notch! One of the first games I got on my VR and love it!! If you are thinking about getting it, send me a message for 25% off (they don’t let us post links on reviews). I’ll send ya the code :)

Seeing games that I've played on Xbox make their way to VR is always exciting. Call of the Sea doesn't disappoint! The puzzles are fun to solve and experiencing the world on Quest is even more exciting! 

Any chance for achievements in a future update?

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your kind words, you made us smile! 
At this moment we don't have plans for adding achievements, sorry for that!

reviews.review_description:
Absolutely love everything about this game.  Well done.  Love the settings and the time period.   
Great story and very creative.
Please make more games like this. Thank You!

I played Call Of The Sea on the PC and loved it. When comparing the VR version, the experience is another thing. I am searching for stuff, grabbing, examining, and WOW, lovely port.  

The decision on some aspects from PC to VR makes sense.

I will only point out that I found some issues when items are on the ground. Even when I crunch, I can't reach the items because I am hitting my floor and haven't reached the item still. It will be nice to have a run feature in a button instead of pressing the left joystick 😛

Overall, I love the experience, I am playing it again, and it feels different; it's time to make the other decision I did not make on the PC 😆.  

Keep it up, dev team 👍

A really great adventure game with super puzzles. The puzzle elements are crucial, but the world is also totally fascinating and much larger and more bombastic than I thought it would be. It's really fun to run and swim in it and solve the puzzles - I felt a bit like Indiana Jones. The puzzles are varied and challenging, but not extremely difficult. There are automatically generated notes to help, and if you still can't progress, there are hints that should allow everyone to solve the puzzles. The story is exciting and well-told. Sound, music, voice acting - everything is top-notch. The graphics could be better, but I was still drawn into this world.

Another game that I could hardly put down until I had finished it ;) Highly recommended for puzzle fans who also enjoy games like Shadowpoint, The Last Clockwinder, A Fisherman's Tale, and similar ones.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review and for sharing your thoughts on our game :)

reviews.review_description: The game is visually great.  The puzzles are nicely challenging. The story is also engaging.  I like the concept of the diary to record the things.  Overall really enjoyed playing the game

Good game overall. Story is a bit out there but enjoyable. Length is good. Some of the puzzles were pretty difficult and I didn’t feel there was enough context to figure out what you needed to do. That could be frustrating and take the fun out of it. I was glad hints were available, however they are more answers than hints. I wish they were just hints. In the first 1 or 2 chapters, the screen would go black for longer periods than I think it was supposed to, and several times the game disappeared and the passthrough came through, so all I saw was my living room. This hasn’t happened with any other game I’ve played. The underwater parts were probably my favorite, however when there was side-to-side movement, it was pretty nauseating. Luckily it doesn’t last too long. Oh man I wasn’t prepared for the end!  That was tough!  Happy playing :)

I’m absolutely loving this game! The graphics are gorgeous and the voice actor is perfect. Totally immersive and just the right amount of difficulty. I like having the hints available. Very unpredictable which I love . Bravo!

This is one of the first games I bought when I got my quest2.  I enjoyed playing it but I notice after playing it for a half hour I become  ￼ dizzy almost like seasickness ￼.  I stopped playing for several days now and ￼a little  ￼ apprehensive to start the game again. ￼
